methods.description: The biochemical analysis was conducted to evaluate the protein, lipid, ash, and carbohydrate content of cultures, as well as to determine the fatty acid profile.

steps.description: All cultures were harvested, centrifuged for 10 min at 2700× g, freeze-dried, and stored at −20°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest and prepare cultures by centrifugation and freeze-drying

steps.description: The protein content was evaluated by elemental analysis of C, H, and N through Vario EL III, according to the manufacturer's procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform elemental analysis to evaluate protein content

steps.description: The total protein content was obtained by the multiplication of the percentage of N by the conversion factor of 4.78 and 4.63 for C. calcitrans and S. costatum, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate total protein content using nitrogen percentage and conversion factors

steps.description: The total lipids were determined according to Bligh and Dyer method, modified by Pereira.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine total lipids using modified Bligh and Dyer method

steps.description: The ash content was determined through gravimetry by burning 50 mg of the samples at 550°C for 8 h, in a furnace.
Carbohydrates were determined by the difference of the sum of total proteins, lipids, and ash content.
The fatty acid profile was determined by using a modification of the Lepage and Roy protocol, described by Pereira.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine fatty acid profile using modified Lepage and Roy protocol
